summary: The primary focus is on osteomas and epidermoid cysts which characterize Gardner syndrome; impacted permanent teeth do not fit within its defining criteria while osteosarcomas arise separately without association with it genetically or clinically. Therefore, Gardner syndrome excludes conditions such as osteosarcoma but includes specific benign bone growths known as osteomas along with various soft tissue masses including desmoids and skin lesions represented by epidermoid cysts. Impacted permanent teeth however fall outside these defined traits making them non-inclusive for diagnosis purposes related directly back to Gardner Syndrome itself due primarily focusing upon gastrointestinal polyps, bony exostoses, cutaneous lipomatosis/desmoids etc., rather than dental anomalies per se. To clarify further regarding inclusion/exclusion based strictly around provided context about 'does NOT INCLUDE', clearly identified exclusions would pertain more towards malignancies unrelated pathogenetically linked via familial adenopolyposis coli mutation carrying state characteristic seen uniquely amongst those diagnosed formally under Gardners' umbrella condition description set forth medically today.
